global_hot_topics.title: TERT drives liver tumorigenesis beyond telomere elongation.
global_hot_topics.authors: Laura Braud; Julien Vernerey; Arnaud Guille; Pierre Cordier; Clémence Ginet; Tom Egger; Manuel Bernabe; Dmitri Churikov
global_hot_topics.abstract: We generated two mouse models, p21+/Tert and p21+/TertCi, expressing either telomerase reverse transcriptase (Tert) or a catalytically inactive variant under the control of the p21 promoter. By 18-20 mo of age, ∼15% of mice from both genotypes developed liver tumors with histopathological features resembling human hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Whole-exome sequencing identified activating Ctnnb1 mutations and recurrent PP1 subunit alterations in p21+/Tert tumors, whereas p21+/TertCi tumors harbored activating HrasGln61Lys mutations associated with elevated C > A transversions. Both models exhibited chromosomal aberrations commonly observed in human HCC. Transcriptomic analyses revealed that β-catenin-activated tumors recapitulated gene expression signatures of human HCC, whereas MAPK-mutated tumors showed profiles consistent with MAPK/ERK pathway activation. All HCCs suppressed the gluconeogenic genes Fbp1 and AldoB, but diverged into two distinct groups based on their glycolytic and NRF2 target gene expression profiles. Spatial profiling further revealed reduced HNF4α-positive hepatocytes across tumors, independent of HNF4α transcription, and markedly diminished immune cell infiltration, particularly in β-catenin-activated tumors. Collectively, these findings uncover telomere-independent functions of Tert and identify molecular and metabolic features with potential relevance for predicting immunotherapy response.
global_hot_topics.title_en: TERT drives liver tumorigenesis beyond telomere elongation.
global_hot_topics.title_cn: TERT 驱动肝脏肿瘤的发生，超出了端粒延长的范围。
global_hot_topics.abstract_cn: 我们生成了两种小鼠模型：p21+/Tert 和 p21+/TertCi，表达端粒酶逆转录酶 (Tert) 或 p21 启动子控制下的催化失活变体。到 18-20 月龄时，两种基因型的小鼠中约有 15% 出现肝脏肿瘤，其组织病理学特征类似于人类肝细胞癌 (HCC)。全外显子组测序发现 p21+/Tert 肿瘤中存在激活的 Ctnnb1 突变和反复发生的 PP1 亚基改变，而 p21+/TertCi 肿瘤则具有与 C > A 颠换升高相关的激活的 HrasGln61Lys 突变。两种模型均表现出人类肝癌中常见的染色体畸变。转录组分析显示，β-连环蛋白激活的肿瘤重现了人类 HCC 的基因表达特征，而 MAPK 突变的肿瘤则显示出与 MAPK/ERK 通路激活一致的特征。所有 HCC 均抑制糖异生基因 Fbp1 和 AldoB，但根据其糖酵解和 NRF2 靶基因表达谱分为两个不同的组。空间分析进一步揭示了肿瘤中 HNF4α 阳性肝细胞的减少，与 HNF4α 转录无关，并且免疫细胞浸润显着减少，特别是在 β-连环蛋白激活的肿瘤中。总的来说，这些发现揭示了 Tert 的独立于端粒的功能，并确定了与预测免疫治疗反应具有潜在相关性的分子和代谢特征。

medical_news.title: A common vitamin could help fight one of the deadliest brain cancers
medical_news.abstract: A clinical trial is exploring whether high doses of vitamin B3 could give patients with glioblastoma a better chance against the aggressive brain cancer. Scientists found that niacin may help revive immune cells that tumors shut down, allowing them to attack cancer more effectively. Early results have been promising, with patients showing significantly better progression-free survival than expected.

medical_news.title_cn: 一种常见的维生素可以帮助对抗最致命的脑癌之一

medical_news.title: New vitamin B12 therapy shows promise against deadly brain cancer
medical_news.abstract: Researchers have identified a vitamin B12–based compound that can cross the blood-brain barrier and home in on glioblastoma tumors. In animal studies, the compound accumulated preferentially in tumor tissue and delivered sustained nitric oxide directly to cancer cells. It also worked synergistically with existing glioblastoma treatments, significantly enhancing their tumor-fighting effects.

medical_news.title: Scientists say creatine may help fight depression
medical_news.abstract: Creatine is best known as a muscle-building supplement, but scientists are now investigating whether it could also help treat depression by boosting the brain's energy supply. A new review examined five randomized clinical trials involving 238 participants and found mixed results. Two studies, both involving women with major depressive disorder, reported that adding creatine to standard treatment improved symptoms, while three others found no meaningful benefit.

medical_news.title: This common vitamin deficiency can mimic normal aging
medical_news.abstract: Vitamin B12 is needed in microscopic amounts, but a shortage can have major effects on health and energy. The vitamin was first linked to a lifesaving liver treatment for pernicious anemia nearly 100 years ago. Today, researchers are finding that B12 may also help keep cellular powerhouses called mitochondria functioning properly. This could explain why some people experience fatigue and brain fog even before traditional signs of deficiency show up.

medical_news.title: Brain activity under anesthesia challenges what we know about consciousness
medical_news.abstract: The unconscious brain appears to be far more capable than scientists once believed. Researchers found that patients under general anesthesia could still process language at a sophisticated level, distinguishing nouns, verbs, and adjectives while listening to stories. Even more remarkably, neural activity showed signs of predicting upcoming words before they were heard. The results challenge traditional ideas about consciousness and hint at new possibilities for brain-computer interfaces.

medical_news.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's kills brain cells
medical_news.abstract: Researchers have identified a previously overlooked mechanism of brain cell death that appears to play a major role in Alzheimer's disease and frontotemporal dementia. The finding could lead to new treatments aimed at slowing neuron loss by interrupting the process before cells are destroyed.
medical_news.title_en: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's kills brain cells
medical_news.title_cn: 科学家可能终于找到了阿尔茨海默病如何杀死脑细胞

medical_news.title: A hidden weakness in deadly cancers could lead to powerful new treatments
medical_news.abstract: A UCLA study has identified a hidden Achilles' heel in aggressive small cell cancers that have resisted new treatments for decades. Scientists found that tumors lacking the RB gene become critically dependent on the protein E2F3 for survival. Blocking E2F3 shut down tumor growth in laboratory models, and existing FDA-approved drugs may be able to exploit this vulnerability. The discovery could pave the way for faster development of more effective therapies.
medical_news.title_en: A hidden weakness in deadly cancers could lead to powerful new treatments
medical_news.title_cn: 致命癌症的隐藏弱点可能会带来强大的新疗法
medical_news.abstract_cn: 加州大学洛杉矶分校的一项研究发现了侵袭性小细胞癌的一个隐藏的致命弱点，这些癌症几十年来一直抵制新疗法。科学家发现，缺乏 RB 基因的肿瘤的生存变得严重依赖于蛋白质 E2F3。阻断 E2F3 可以阻止实验室模型中的肿瘤生长，而 FDA 批准的现有药物可能能够利用这一漏洞。这一发现可能为更快开发更有效的疗法铺平道路。

medical_news.title: Exoskeleton Reads Faint Muscle Signals to Overcome Hand Paralysis
medical_news.abstract: A new study introduces an affordable, soft pneumatic exoskeleton glove that restores grasping function in individuals with hand paralysis. Driven by the eXprt innovation network, the system uses machine learning to decode forearm EMG signals with 97% reliability, allowing an ALS patient to grasp objects and hold a fork for the first time in four years.
medical_news.title_en: Exoskeleton Reads Faint Muscle Signals to Overcome Hand Paralysis
medical_news.title_cn: 外骨骼读取微弱的肌肉信号以克服手部麻痹
medical_news.abstract_cn: 一项新研究介绍了一种价格实惠、柔软的气动外骨骼手套，可以恢复手部瘫痪患者的抓握功能。在 eXprt 创新网络的驱动下，该系统利用机器学习以 97% 的可靠性解码前臂 EMG 信号，使 ALS 患者四年来首次能够抓握物体并握住叉子。
